thread_title: Just diagnosed with ADHD—I have no idea what to do next
thread_description: Got the report from the psych yesterday. 15 pages I don't understand, recommendations I can't afford, and a label that somehow feels like relief AND shame at the same time.
opening_story: I spent 6 months fighting to get this evaluation. Now I'm frozen. Where do I actually start?
invitation_questions: What was the first thing you did after diagnosis that actually helped? (Not what you were supposed to do—what you actually did)

thread_title: My family thinks this is an excuse to be lazy
thread_description: Got diagnosed with ADHD. My dad's response: 'You're just not trying hard enough.' My mom: 'This generation relies too much on labels.' My spouse is skeptical too.
opening_story: Everyone around me reacted with doubt, and now I'm wondering if they're right. Is this diagnosis real or am I just... broken?
invitation_questions: How did your family react to your (or your kid's) diagnosis? How did you handle it without losing your mind?

thread_title: This diagnosis explains my entire life
thread_description: I'm 34. Just diagnosed ADHD. Everything clicks now—my job failures, relationships, why I can't do simple tasks. I feel relieved but also devastated I wasted 30+ years.
opening_story: All these years I thought I was lazy, broken, unmotivated. Turns out I'm just ADHD. Now what?
invitation_questions: If you got diagnosed later in life, how did you process it? Did it change how you see yourself?

thread_title: The diagnosis is real but the help is impossible to find
thread_description: Psych confirmed ADHD. They referred me to a therapist. Therapist said 'Call back in 3 months.' Medication clinic has a 6-month wait. Google says 'try meditation' and 'eat protein.'
opening_story: I have a diagnosis. I also have zero access to actual treatment. Do I just... live with this now?
invitation_questions: How did you find actual support after diagnosis? What resources actually exist in India that are accessible?

thread_title: Should I tell everyone or keep it private?
thread_description: Got diagnosed last week. Now I'm terrified. If I tell people at work, will they see me differently? If I tell family, will they use it against me? If I keep it quiet, am I lying?
opening_story: The diagnosis is mine but it doesn't feel safe to own it out loud.
invitation_questions: How did you decide who to tell? What did you wish you'd known before you told someone?

thread_title: I think I have it but everyone says I don't
thread_description: I'm convinced I'm ADHD/autistic. My doctor says I'm 'fine' because I have a job and don't fail classes. But I'm struggling so much internally—is that not enough?
opening_story: My experience is valid but apparently not *diagnosable* enough.
invitation_questions: How did you get a diagnosis when people around you said you were fine? What changed?

thread_title: One diagnosis explained, now I'm confused if I have the other one too
thread_description: Son diagnosed with autism. Now I'm reading about ADHD and going 'wait, do I have this too?' Where does one condition end and the other begin?
opening_story: How do I know if this is two separate things or one puzzle with overlapping pieces?
invitation_questions: If you have multiple diagnoses (or wonder if you do), how did you figure that out? How does it change your understanding of yourself?

thread_title: The guilt is crushing me
thread_description: If I hadn't missed the diagnosis for my kid for 7 years, they wouldn't have struggled so much. If I'd recognized it in myself earlier, my marriage might have survived. The regret is suffocating.
opening_story: I can't stop replaying what I could've done differently.
invitation_questions: How do you move past the guilt of a delayed diagnosis? How do you forgive yourself?

thread_title: I finally understand why school was hell
thread_description: I was diagnosed with ADHD at 25. Looking back at my school trauma—the shame, the punishment, the feeling that I was 'bad'—I realize my teachers didn't understand. They just saw me as a problem.
opening_story: All those years of feeling broken in a classroom designed for a different brain.
invitation_questions: If you were undiagnosed at school and then diagnosed later, how did you reframe those memories?

thread_title: Is this medication safe for my kid?
thread_description: Doctor recommended ADHD meds for my 7yo. Everything I read online says it's dangerous/addictive/will ruin their brain. But untreated ADHD is also affecting them. I'm paralyzed.
opening_story: I trust the doctor but I also trust the scary Reddit threads.
invitation_questions: If your child is on medication, what was your decision-making process? What actually happened (not the fears)?

thread_title: My kid got diagnosed but won't talk about it
thread_description: My 12yo got diagnosed with autism. They refuse to acknowledge it, call it stupid, say everyone's a little autistic. I'm not forcing it but the denial worries me.
opening_story: They have the diagnosis. They're not accepting it. Now what?
invitation_questions: How do you support someone (especially a kid) who has a diagnosis they don't want to claim?

thread_title: Diagnosis was a relief but my parents are grieving
thread_description: They reacted to my diagnosis like I told them I was dying. They're sad, angry, mourning the 'normal' child they thought they had. Meanwhile I'm just feeling... seen for the first time.
invitation_questions: How do you navigate your own relief when the people who raised you are grieving?

thread_title: I can't afford the next steps after diagnosis
thread_description: Got diagnosed. Great. Now: therapy costs ₹3000/month. Medication evaluation costs ₹5000. Speech therapy (recommended for my 5yo) costs ₹2000/session. Our grocery budget can't stretch.
opening_story: Diagnosis is free if you go to government hospital. Everything else costs more than rent.
invitation_questions: What did you do when the diagnosis came with bills you couldn't pay? What actually works without money?

thread_title: Everyone's telling me to 'embrace neurodiversity' but I'm just scared
thread_description: I've read all the 'ADHD is a superpower' posts. Cool. I'm still terrified about what my future looks like, whether I can hold a job, whether anyone will love me.
opening_story: Acceptance feels far away when survival feels uncertain.
invitation_questions: What helped you move from fear to something like acceptance? (Not positivity—just peace.)

thread_title: The pressure to be 'high-functioning' is killing me
thread_description: I got diagnosed ADHD but because I 'seem fine' (I mask very well), people minimize it. 'You don't look ADHD,' 'You have a job so you must be exaggerating.' The exhaustion of being high-functioning is what landed me in crisis.
opening_story: I'm fine until I'm not. Then I crash hard.
invitation_questions: If you're 'high-functioning' but struggling, how do you make people understand it's still real?

thread_title: My diagnosis has become my entire identity
thread_description: Six months post-diagnosis and I've joined every ADHD group, read every book, reorganized my life around accommodations. I'm grateful but also—when do I stop being 'the ADHD person' and just be me?
opening_story: I swapped one box (broken) for another (ADHD).
invitation_questions: How do you integrate a diagnosis without letting it become your whole personality?

thread_title: I was diagnosed but I don't feel different
thread_description: Officially ADHD. I expected some revelation. But I wake up and feel exactly the same. All those struggles are still there. The diagnosis alone didn't fix anything.
opening_story: I thought knowing would change everything. It didn't.
invitation_questions: Did getting diagnosed feel like a turning point or an anticlimax? How long until things actually started shifting?

thread_title: My kid got diagnosed and everyone's suddenly an expert
thread_description: We went public with our kid's ADHD diagnosis (had to for school accommodations). Now random people give unsolicited advice: 'You should try this diet,' 'My nephew's cousin was cured with yoga,' 'You're medicating your child wrong.'
opening_story: I asked for accommodations, not commentary.
invitation_questions: How do you handle people's opinions about your kid's diagnosis? How do you protect your family's privacy?

thread_title: The diagnosis confirmed my deepest fear
thread_description: I always knew something was 'wrong' with me. The diagnosis just made it official. Now I have proof I'm not normal, and that's both validating and devastating.
opening_story: Knowing is better than not knowing. But it's also lonelier.
invitation_questions: How do you sit with the reality of a diagnosis you half-expected but still doesn't feel good?

thread_title: Nobody warned me about post-diagnosis depression
thread_description: Got diagnosed ADHD at 30. First week was relief. By week 3, I was depressed. Grieving all the ways this affected my life before I knew. Is this normal?
opening_story: The clarity feels like opening a wound.
invitation_questions: Did you experience depression or grief after diagnosis? When did it lift?

thread_title: Should we test our other child?
thread_description: Older kid diagnosed with autism. Younger one is 'fine' but shows some similar traits. Do we get them tested or let them be? Are we creating labels unnecessarily?
opening_story: One diagnosis feels like opening a door to finding diagnoses everywhere.
invitation_questions: If multiple kids in your family are neurodivergent, how did you decide who to test and when?

thread_title: The therapist I found is hopeless
thread_description: Diagnosis psych was great. Referred me to 'an ADHD specialist' to help with next steps. This therapist has no idea about ADHD, keeps trying to analyze my childhood, and dismisses my diagnosis as 'a label.'
opening_story: I thought getting the diagnosis was the hard part. Finding the right support is harder.
invitation_questions: How did you find a good therapist after diagnosis? Red flags to watch for?

thread_title: My spouse still doesn't believe it's real
thread_description: I have a diagnosis. My spouse thinks I'm making it an excuse. This is creating a chasm in our marriage. They think if I try harder or stop 'using ADHD' as a crutch, I'd be fine.
opening_story: I have proof. They have doubt. We're stuck.
invitation_questions: How did you get your partner on board with the diagnosis? Or did you have to move forward without their belief?

thread_title: Now I understand why I never fit in—and I'm grieving that
thread_description: ADHD diagnosis at 35. My whole life makes sense now. Except... I'm angry. All those years of trying to be someone else, of failing, of shame. The clarity doesn't erase the pain.
opening_story: Diagnosis is answer and accusation at the same time.
invitation_questions: If you were undiagnosed for most of your life, how do you process the loss of a 'normal' path you never had?

thread_title: I need help but I don't know what to ask for
thread_description: Diagnosed last month. I know something needs to change but I don't know if it's therapy, medication, coaching, school accommodations, job changes, or all of it. Where do I even start?
invitation_questions: After diagnosis, what was your first actual step? Not what you thought you should do—what you did?

thread_title: The diagnosis is real but my life hasn't changed
thread_description: Got diagnosed 3 months ago. Still struggling the same way. Diagnosis alone wasn't the fix. Now I'm paying for support and it's slow. People ask 'are you better yet?' and the answer is 'no, but now I understand why.'
opening_story: Knowledge without action is just pain with context.
invitation_questions: What's been the timeline of actual change in your life post-diagnosis? When did things actually shift?

thread_title: My 4yo has a meltdown every morning before school
thread_description: Wake-up to uniform to car to classroom in 15 minutes = complete dysregulation. Not because they don't like school—because transitions shred their nervous system.
opening_story: By the time we reach school, they're crying, I'm frustrated, teacher thinks it's a behavior problem. Nobody understands it's a regulation issue.
invitation_questions: What morning routine/transition hack actually works? How do you advocate for understanding at school?

thread_title: Therapy costs more than I make
thread_description: Speech therapy (₹2000/session): 2x/week. Occupational therapy (₹1500/session): 2x/week. That's ₹14k/month. My salary is ₹40k. I'm hemorrhaging money while my kid gets zero therapy.
opening_story: Early intervention is critical. Also impossible. Pick one.
invitation_questions: How are you affording therapy? Subsidized options? DIY? Government resources?

thread_title: The pediatrician dismissed everything
thread_description: I mentioned my 5yo's sensory issues and speech delay. Pediatrician said 'boys are just slower at talking' and 'all kids hate tags.' When I pushed, they said I was anxious. Delayed my evaluation by a year.
opening_story: Trust your gut when professionals dismiss you.
invitation_questions: How did you advocate for evaluation when doctors said 'wait and see'?

thread_title: My in-laws think it's bad parenting, not ADHD
thread_description: Kid diagnosed ADHD. MIL's reaction: 'We never had ADHD in our time. You're just not disciplining properly.' Now family gatherings are full of subtle jabs.
opening_story: The diagnosis is mine. The shame is family-inflicted.
invitation_questions: How do you handle family members who invalidate the diagnosis? How do you protect your kid from their judgment?

thread_title: My kid stims and everyone stares
thread_description: My 3yo flaps hands, spins, makes repetitive sounds—all regulation tools. But in public (grocery store, park, temple), strangers stare. Comments like 'is your child okay?' make my kid feel watched and wrong.
opening_story: My kid's regulation is being interrupted by society's judgment.
invitation_questions: How do you raise an autistic/ADHD child in public without internalizing other people's discomfort?

thread_title: Nobody at school understands my kid's needs
thread_description: School says: 'Your child is fine here.' But they're masking hard. At home, they're dysregulated all evening. I know school is causing distress but proving it requires documentation, meeting, fighting.
opening_story: My kid performs normal for 6 hours then crashes. School calls this success.
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for accommodations when the school says your kid 'seems fine'?

thread_title: I don't know how to parent a neurodivergent brain
thread_description: Parenting advice (time-outs, reward charts, consistency) works for my NT kid. With my ND kid, these strategies backfire. I'm reading books, watching videos, and still feel lost.
opening_story: Everything I learned about parenting doesn't work.
invitation_questions: What parenting strategies actually work for your neurodivergent kid? What does *connection* look like for them?

thread_title: Extended family doesn't get the diagnosis at all
thread_description: Parents understand ADHD. Grandparents are baffled. 'In our day, we just made kids sit still,' 'You're overcomplicating it,' 'Stop looking for excuses.' Family visits are full of tension.
invitation_questions: How do you teach extended family about your child's diagnosis without exhausting yourself?

thread_title: My sensory kid can't wear regular clothes
thread_description: Seams cause meltdowns. Tags are torture. Regular uniforms are a nightmare. Special clothes exist but they're expensive. Most people can't fathom why a tag matters so much.
opening_story: Something so small for most people is a daily crisis for mine.
invitation_questions: How do you navigate sensory clothing needs at school and in daily life?

thread_title: Bedtime is a two-hour nightmare
thread_description: Bedtime routine: bath (sensory overload), pajamas (uncomfortable), lying still (impossible for ADHD kid), darkness (anxiety). By lights-out, both of us are exhausted and resentful.
opening_story: Every parent struggles with bedtime. For neurodivergent kids, it's a sensory/regulation crisis.
invitation_questions: What bedtime routine actually works? What did you give up trying?

thread_title: I'm burnt out before 9am
thread_description: Get kid out of bed (meltdown). Brush teeth (resistance). Eat breakfast (texture issues). Get dressed (sensory meltdown). School by 8:30am = I'm already emotionally exhausted before the day starts.
opening_story: Before breakfast, I've done 5 emotional crises.
invitation_questions: How do you structure mornings without losing your mind? What's your non-negotiable vs. flexible?

thread_title: Food is a sensory battle
thread_description: My kid will eat: chicken, rice, specific brand of yogurt. Everything else = texture gag, tears. Nutritionist says they're fine but I worry about nutrition and social eating.
opening_story: So much power is wrapped up in feeding a selective eater.
invitation_questions: How do you manage sensory food aversion without making mealtimes a war?

thread_title: My 6yo won't talk but everyone says 'they'll talk when they're ready'
thread_description: Speech delay. Not nonverbal but significantly behind. Everyone: 'boys talk later,' 'Albert Einstein didn't talk till 3,' 'stop worrying.' But waiting is costing time, and I can't push for evaluation because apparently I'm anxious.
opening_story: Trusting your gut is harder when everyone contradicts it.
invitation_questions: How did you push for speech evaluation when people dismissed it? What changed?

thread_title: Playdate? No thanks, too much chaos
thread_description: My ADHD kid can't handle the stimulation of other kids. Even 30 minutes of peer play = dysregulation for hours after. But kids need socialization and I'm exhausted hiding away.
opening_story: Isolation feels safer than the dysregulation that follows.
invitation_questions: How do you help your kid build friendships when group settings are overwhelming?

thread_title: I feel guilty for medicating my child
thread_description: Pediatrician recommended ADHD medication. It genuinely helps my kid focus. But I feel guilty: Am I taking the easy way out? Should I try harder with behavior strategies? Is medication 'cheating'?
opening_story: I know it helps. Shame says I'm failing by using it.
invitation_questions: If your child is on medication, how did you get past the guilt? How do you know it's the right choice?

thread_title: My kid stims and I'm told to stop it
thread_description: Teacher asked me to work on reducing hand-flapping (my kid's regulation tool). School sees it as a 'behavior issue' not a regulation need. Do I comply with school or honor my kid's needs?
opening_story: The tools my kid uses to regulate are being pathologized by adults.
invitation_questions: How do you protect your kid's natural stims while navigating school pressure to suppress them?

thread_title: I'm losing my marriage to parenting an ND child
thread_description: All my energy goes to managing my kid's needs. My spouse feels invisible. Intimacy is gone. We fight about parenting, about fairness, about whose responsibility it is. The marriage is cracking.
opening_story: One child's needs are becoming a couple's crisis.
invitation_questions: How do you stay connected to your partner while parenting an ND child? How do you divide labor fairly?

thread_title: I don't know what my kid will even be able to do someday
thread_description: My 4yo was just diagnosed. I watch other kids and imagine their futures. Mine? Unknown. Sometimes I can't see past tomorrow.
opening_story: Diagnosis forces you to face uncertainty you didn't want to contemplate yet.
invitation_questions: How do you sit with the unknown of your child's future without catastrophizing?

thread_title: Everyone's advice contradicts everyone else's advice
thread_description: One expert: 'strict routine is essential.' Another: 'forcing routine creates rigidity.' Third: 'your child needs flexibility.' I'm following all of them and none of them work.
invitation_questions: Whose advice do you actually follow? How do you filter the noise?

thread_title: My kid's meltdown looks like 'bad behavior' to observers
thread_description: Full-body dysregulation meltdown in the grocery store. Strangers judge me. 'Why can't you control your child?' I'm not trying to control anything—I'm trying to help them regulate.
opening_story: My kid is dysregulated. The world sees a discipline failure.
invitation_questions: How do you protect your kid from public judgment during dysregulation? How do you handle it in the moment?

thread_title: I'm the only one who understands my kid
thread_description: My kid is perfect with me, dysregulated with everyone else. Teachers, grandparents, therapists—all say 'they're fine here, maybe it's you.' But I see the effort my kid is making to hold it together.
opening_story: Nobody else has full visibility to the effort.
invitation_questions: How do you help others see what you see? Or do you stop trying?

thread_title: Diagnosis didn't come with a manual
thread_description: Got the diagnosis. Now what? No one explained: how do we access accommodations? What's realistic to expect? How do I parent differently? Where do I even START?
opening_story: Diagnosis is answer but not roadmap.
invitation_questions: After your child's diagnosis, what was your first concrete step? What resources actually helped?

thread_title: Board exams + ADHD = perfect storm
thread_description: 10th board exams starting in 4 months. My child's ADHD makes: sitting for 3 hours = impossible, reading dense material = torture, time management = nonexistent, test anxiety = paralysis.
opening_story: Standard exam structure is designed for a different brain.
invitation_questions: How are you preparing your teen for boards with ADHD? Are they getting accommodations? How do you actually make exams work?

thread_title: School won't give accommodations for boards even though the law allows it
thread_description: RPWD Act allows scribes + extra time for board exams. Our school says: 'Never done it,' 'Not possible,' 'You need to fight the board directly.' Three months away and nothing's in place.
opening_story: The law exists. Schools ignore it.
invitation_questions: Have you gotten board exam accommodations? How did you make the school comply?

thread_title: My teen is lonely and doesn't know how to fix it
thread_description: Bright, funny, kind—but zero close friendships. Sits alone at lunch. Misses social cues, makes jokes that land wrong, can't read group dynamics. I watch them suffer and feel helpless.
opening_story: Loneliness at 14 is devastating in ways adults can solve differently.
invitation_questions: How do you support a lonely teen with social challenges? What actually helps vs. makes it worse?

thread_title: Masking is destroying my teen from the inside
thread_description: My 13yo acts 'normal' at school (exhaustingly). At home: dysregulation, meltdowns, zero energy. I can see the exhaustion of maintaining a fake self all day. But if they stop masking, school becomes hostile.
opening_story: They're 13 and already burnt out from performing normalcy.
invitation_questions: If your teen is masking heavily, how do you help them? Do you push for unmasking or help them mask better?

thread_title: My teen was just diagnosed—they're refusing to accept it
thread_description: Diagnosis: autism. Teen's reaction: 'I'm not autistic,' 'Everyone's a little autistic,' 'This label is stupid.' They're rejecting the diagnosis and me for suggesting it helps.
opening_story: They have proof they're neurodivergent but won't claim it.
invitation_questions: How do you support a teen who won't accept their own diagnosis?

thread_title: Nobody at school sees the struggle because they mask perfectly
thread_description: Teen: straight A's, popular-adjacent, no visible struggles. But at home: anxiety, depression, sensory overload, executive function collapse. School thinks they're fine. I know they're one crisis away from breakdown.
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for help when your teen 'looks fine'? How do you name the hidden struggle?

thread_title: My teen wants ADHD diagnosis but I'm skeptical
thread_description: They self-diagnosed online. We got tested; turns out they're right. But I'm struggling with: is this real or TikTok trends? Am I medicating normal teen struggle?
opening_story: Teen knows themselves. Parent doubt creates second-guessing.
invitation_questions: How do you resolve your own doubt about your teen's diagnosis?

thread_title: Peer rejection is becoming bullying and the school won't act
thread_description: My 8th grader has ADHD, awkward socially. Peers mock them daily. School response: 'They're being kids,' 'They need to learn to handle it.' But my teen is becoming suicidal about it.
invitation_questions: How do you escalate when school won't protect your child from bullying?

thread_title: My teen is questioning their identity (and it's tied to neurodivergence)
thread_description: ADHD teen realizing they might not be cisgender or straight. Identity questions + neurodivergence = complex. They're trying to understand who they are and whether neurodivergence affects it.
opening_story: Adolescence is identity crisis on a normal day. Add neurodivergence.
invitation_questions: How do you support a teen exploring identity while neurodivergent?

thread_title: Study groups don't work for my ADHD teen
thread_description: Everyone else studies in groups. My teen gets distracted, anxious, overstimulated. They study best alone with stimulation (fidgeting, music, movement). But this feels isolating and teachers question it.
invitation_questions: How does your neurodivergent teen actually study? What works when group study doesn't?

thread_title: My teen is in romantic crisis and I have no script
thread_description: They like someone. That person is 'busy.' My teen can't read the actual situation (are they interested? uninterested? slow-burn? over?). Anxiety is eating them alive and I have no framework to help.
opening_story: Romance and ADHD/autism = recipe for misreading + heartbreak.
invitation_questions: How do you help your teen navigate dating and crushes when they misread social cues?

thread_title: My teen used to love learning and now hates school
thread_description: ADHD teen thrived in elementary (looser structure, play-based). Middle school (rigid, fast, complex social scene, abstract learning) = shutdown. They went from excited to 'I hate school' in one year.
opening_story: Different developmental stage + different learning environment = disaster.
invitation_questions: How do you reignite a teen's relationship with learning after burnout?

thread_title: My teen's mood swings have me terrified
thread_description: ADHD/autism + puberty hormones = mood dysregulation that's scary. One moment fine, next moment rage or despair. I don't know if this is normal puberty or mental health crisis.
invitation_questions: How do you distinguish between normal teen moodiness and concerning dysregulation?

thread_title: She masks at school but I can see her unraveling
thread_description: My 7th grade daughter seems fine at school (teachers say she's great). But I see: anxiety about perceived social failures, exhaustion, perfectionism that's crushing, sensory sensitivity escalating.
opening_story: Girls are exceptional at hiding. Until they break.
invitation_questions: How do you support a teen who's masking and you can see the cost?

thread_title: My teen won't do homework and everyone thinks it's laziness
thread_description: Executive function challenges + homework demand = stand-off. Teen wants to pass, isn't lazy, literally cannot initiate and complete the work. Teachers see refusal.
opening_story: Neurodivergent struggle looks like defiance to people who don't understand.
invitation_questions: How do you help your teen with homework when executive function is the barrier? What actually works?

thread_title: Medication makes them 'different' and they hate it
thread_description: ADHD meds help my teen focus but also make them feel 'flat,' less like themselves. They want to stop. I worry if they stop, they'll crash academically.
opening_story: Helps with one thing, costs something else.
invitation_questions: If your teen is on meds and having identity/side effect concerns, how are you navigating it?

thread_title: My teen is isolating and I can't force connection
thread_description: They have interests nobody else shares, don't fit peer culture, prefer solitude. Some of this is healthy introversion. Some feels like depression. How do I know the difference?
invitation_questions: How do you know if your teen's isolation is healthy or concerning? What helps?

thread_title: Summer is hell—unstructured time breaks my teen
thread_description: School structure holds them together. Summer (no school, no routine, open days) = dysregulation, screen addiction, mood crash. But every 'solution' I try is rejected.
invitation_questions: How do you create structure and purpose for ADHD/autistic teens in summer without it feeling forced?

thread_title: My teen's diagnosis changes their college prospects and I'm grieving
thread_description: Mild learning disability + ADHD. College will be hard. Competitive schools are closed. My grief about the ceiling this puts on their options is real, and I feel like a bad parent for grieving.
invitation_questions: How do you grieve the 'normal' path your teen won't have while supporting them in the actual one they will?

thread_title: Peer pressure affects them differently (and worse)
thread_description: NT kids have peer pressure (dress right, be cool). My ADHD teen has peer pressure PLUS doesn't understand social subtext. They're vulnerable to being taken advantage of in ways other kids aren't.
invitation_questions: How do you teach your teen about safe relationships and boundaries when they don't read social cues?

thread_title: My teen is self-harming and I'm terrified
thread_description: Cutting, scratching, head-banging during dysregulation. School is notified, therapist is involved, but I'm living in terror. Is this ADHD + teen crisis or early-stage mental illness?
invitation_questions: How do you support a teen who's self-harming? What actually helps when the urge to fix it is overwhelming?

thread_title: Everyone has advice and none of it helps
thread_description: 'Set firmer boundaries.' 'Stop being so strict.' 'Medicate.' 'Don't medicate.' 'Do therapy.' 'Therapy is a waste.' 'They'll grow out of it.' Each person's certain they know the right answer. I know my kid.
opening_story: Too many voices, zero true understanding.
invitation_questions: Whose advice actually resonates? How do you filter?

thread_title: My teen is brilliant but school is punishing them for differences
thread_description: High IQ, extreme focus in interests, creative problem-solver—but can't sit still during irrelevant material, refuses 'busy work,' questions every rule. Teacher calls it 'defiance.' It's intelligence + ADHD.
opening_story: Schools designed to kill curiosity in neurodivergent kids.
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for a gifted neurodivergent teen when school misses their potential?

thread_title: My teen's therapist doesn't understand neurodivergence
thread_description: Therapist keeps trying to 'fix' ADHD-related behavior (procrastination, time blindness) through willpower and shame. Not understanding that it's neurological, not character flaw. Teen stopped going.
opening_story: Wrong therapist is worse than no therapist.
invitation_questions: How do you find a therapist who actually understands ADHD/autism, especially for teens?

thread_title: My teen's shame about their diagnosis is becoming identity
thread_description: They see ADHD/autism as what makes them broken, not as part of who they are. Shame is affecting everything: friendships, academics, how they see their future.
opening_story: Diagnosis has become self-definition, but the definition is 'broken.'
invitation_questions: How do you help your teen reframe their diagnosis from shame to self-understanding?

thread_title: Do I tell my employer or keep it secret?
thread_description: Just diagnosed ADHD at 28. Should I disclose at work? Pros: possible accommodations. Cons: discrimination (illegal but happens), career stalling, judgment. How do people in India navigate this?
opening_story: Proof in pocket, but is it safe to name it?
invitation_questions: If you disclosed at work in India, what actually happened? Was it worth it?

thread_title: Explaining employment gaps without outing myself
thread_description: Six jobs in seven years. Each lasted 1–3 years before burnout. Now on the job market and resume screams 'unstable.' How do I tell the real story (undiagnosed ADHD = burnout) without weaponizing vulnerability?
invitation_questions: How did you reframe your employment history on your resume or in interviews?

thread_title: I mask at work and it's killing me
thread_description: By 5pm, I'm exhausted. Masking 8+ hours (appearing focused, organized, socially smooth) leaves nothing left. I come home and can't do anything. This isn't sustainable.
invitation_questions: How do you reduce masking at work without risking your job? Is it even possible in corporate India?

thread_title: My ADHD makes me brilliant at some things, terrible at others
thread_description: I'm exceptional at: design thinking, seeing patterns, generating ideas, hyperfocus. Terrible at: follow-through, documentation, meetings, deadlines. How do I build a career that emphasizes strengths and minimizes weaknesses?
invitation_questions: How have you built a role around your actual brain instead of forcing your brain into standard roles?

thread_title: I got diagnosed late and I'm grieving my career
thread_description: Diagnosed at 35. Looking back: I took jobs that didn't fit, burnt out repeatedly, self-blamed, thought I was failing. A different diagnosis earlier = different path. Now what?
opening_story: Diagnosis is clarity and loss at once.
invitation_questions: How do you accept a different career reality than you imagined?

thread_title: Remote work saved my life; office wants us back
thread_description: Remote work: I'm thriving. No commute sensory overload, can regulate breaks, work during peak focus times. Company wants in-office 3 days/week. The thought makes me want to quit.
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for remote work when it's essential for you?

thread_title: My manager doesn't get it and I don't know how to explain
thread_description: I said I work better with flex deadlines and 25-min focused sprints (ADHD rhythm). Manager: 'That's not how professionals work.' Now I'm afraid to ask for accommodations.
opening_story: Manager's worldview doesn't include neurodivergent working styles.
invitation_questions: How do you educate a resistant manager without feeling like you're begging?

thread_title: I'm brilliant in my niche but 'unemployable' in standard roles
thread_description: I've built freelance work in my hyperfocus area (photography, coding, design). It pays less and has no stability, but I thrive. Corporate paths close to me because I don't 'seem professional enough.'
opening_story: Niche = perfect fit and fragile income.
invitation_questions: How do you build sustainable self-employment as a neurodivergent adult?

thread_title: Job interviews trigger every ADHD symptom
thread_description: Sit in waiting room = anxiety. Eye contact = paralysis. Speed questions with thoughtful answers = impossible. Assessment tests = disaster. I fail interviews even when I'd excel at the actual job.
opening_story: System designed for a different brain.
invitation_questions: How do you get past interviews when ADHD makes them a nightmare? Any hacks?

thread_title: My untreated ADHD is affecting my marriage
thread_description: I'm late constantly. I forget important dates. I hyperfocus on work and ignore my spouse. They're resentful. Diagnosis (after marriage took damage) changed things but the hurt is still there.
invitation_questions: If ADHD affected your relationships before diagnosis, how did you repair things?

thread_title: Burnout turned into depression and I'm not sure I can work anymore
thread_description: Pushed hard for 10 years. Burnt out catastrophically. Now even thinking about work triggers depression. Disability leave ended. I need income but can't imagine job hunting.
invitation_questions: How have you rebuilt your relationship with work after burnout?

thread_title: I'm doing everything right but still imposter syndrome
thread_description: I have ADHD diagnosis, accommodations in place, therapist, good job. But I still feel like I'll be found out. Like I don't deserve this. Is this ADHD or just me?
invitation_questions: How do you manage imposter syndrome when your diagnosis tries to convince you it's real?

thread_title: Financial disaster from years of ADHD decisions
thread_description: Impulsive purchases, missed bills (literally forgot to pay them), salary management chaos. Now in debt and don't know how to build financial stability when your brain doesn't track money.
invitation_questions: How do you build financial stability with ADHD? What systems actually work?

thread_title: I'm socially terrible at work and it's limiting my growth
thread_description: I miss social cues in meetings, make awkward jokes, can't small talk, say things I regret. Good work doesn't compensate for social fumbles. Colleagues don't take me seriously.
invitation_questions: How do you navigate workplace social dynamics when you miss the unspoken rules?

thread_title: Should I disclose to my team or just my manager?
thread_description: Planning to disclose ADHD. Manager is supportive. Team is... unknown. If I tell them, will they lower expectations? If I don't, will they think I'm lazy/flaky?
opening_story: Disclosure is power given to people.
invitation_questions: How much detail should you share with whom? How do you control the narrative?

thread_title: Medication helps but people think I'm addicted
thread_description: Taking ADHD meds and they're genuinely useful (focus, mood stability). But colleagues: 'Aren't you worried about addiction?' Family: 'You're becoming dependent.' The stigma is isolating.
opening_story: Legitimate treatment is treated like habit.
invitation_questions: How do you handle stigma around ADHD medication?

thread_title: I'm building something of my own (freelance/startup)
thread_description: Got tired of corporate jobs (masking, burnout, structure mismatch). Building freelance consulting. It's aligned with my ADHD (variety, autonomy, hyperfocus) but also precarious (income, benefits, isolation).
invitation_questions: How do you build sustainable self-employment as a neurodivergent adult?

thread_title: My diagnosis explained why certain careers never stuck
thread_description: I've cycled through: sales, engineering, project management, HR. Each time: initial excitement, then burnout. Pattern invisible until diagnosis. Now I'm finally pursuing a path (creative work) that actually fits my brain.
invitation_questions: How did your diagnosis shift your understanding of your career arc?

thread_title: Living paycheck-to-paycheck after years of chaos
thread_description: Undiagnosed ADHD + job instability + poor money management = perpetual financial stress. Diagnosis helps me understand it, but I'm still broke.
invitation_questions: How are you building financial stability? What actually helps when income is uncertain?

thread_title: My ADHD makes me unreliable to people I care about
thread_description: I forget plans, cancel last-minute, am late constantly. Friends are pulling back. My ADHD is being read as disrespect. How do I explain time-blindness and executive function without making excuses?
invitation_questions: How do you maintain friendships when ADHD makes you a flaky friend?

thread_title: Career success at the cost of everything else
thread_description: I've climbed the ladder (hyperfocus + masking). But: no intimate relationships, no hobbies, no life outside work. Success feels hollow. Can I have both?
invitation_questions: How do you build a life that isn't just career? How do you break the achievement spiral?

thread_title: My student can't sit still and everyone thinks it's a behavior problem
thread_description: 7th-grade student: constant fidgeting, calls out, interrupts, can't sit for more than 10 minutes. I've tried seating changes, behavior charts, consequences. Nothing works. Is this ADHD or am I just a bad teacher?
invitation_questions: What's helped you distinguish between willful misbehavior and dysregulation?

thread_title: I have no training in inclusive education but I'm expected to provide it
thread_description: 40 students in class. One student is autistic, one has ADHD, two have learning disabilities. No resources, no training, no support. Teacher expectations: teach everyone at pace, all at same level.
invitation_questions: How do you differentiate for 40 kids with zero support?

thread_title: Parent wants accommodations I can't provide
thread_description: Request: separate invigilation for exams, extended time, alternate assessment format. School policy doesn't allow it. Legal framework says we should. I'm caught in the middle.
invitation_questions: How have you navigated accommodations when school systems resist?

thread_title: My 'problem student' might be autistic—what do I do?
thread_description: Student: quiet in class (because overwhelmed), selective about participation, struggles with group work, prefers predictable routines. No diagnosis. Parent doesn't see it. How do I suggest evaluation without offending?
invitation_questions: How do you raise concerns about a student's possible neurodivergence professionally?

thread_title: Sensory issues aren't taken seriously in mainstream school
thread_description: Student's sensory needs: can't wear uniform (seams), needs movement breaks, gets overwhelmed in noisy classroom. Response: 'Every student adapts to school, not the other way around.' Policy: no exceptions.
invitation_questions: How do you work within systems that won't acknowledge sensory issues?

thread_title: I'm supporting a neurodivergent student but their home doesn't understand
thread_description: Student thrives at school (accommodations, structure, consistency). But at home: parents minimize diagnosis, don't provide consistency, resist medication. School support is undermined at home.
opening_story: Your support ends at the school gate.
invitation_questions: How do you partner with families who don't accept their child's neurodivergence?

thread_title: My classroom strategies aren't in any teacher training manual
thread_description: I've figured out: visual schedules reduce transitions, movement breaks improve focus, fidget tools don't distract (they help), small group work > large group for some students. These work but I'm making it up.
invitation_questions: What classroom strategies do you use that actually work, even though they weren't in your training?

thread_title: I'm out of patience and that's affecting my teaching
thread_description: Teaching 40 kids, many with undiagnosed needs, zero support, high pressure. Fatigue is turning into frustration, and frustration is showing in how I treat struggling students. I'm not the teacher I want to be.
invitation_questions: How do you preserve compassion when you're burnt out?

thread_title: Board exam accommodations feel impossible to coordinate
thread_description: Law says I should facilitate: scribes, extra time, separate rooms. Reality: school has no budget, no trained scribes, exam logistics are rigid. How do I make this work?
invitation_questions: How have you actually implemented board exam accommodations?

thread_title: Some students hide everything and I miss it
thread_description: High-achieving student seems fine. Report card shows struggle. Parents say 'doing okay.' But student is masking hard, anxious, and on the edge of breakdown. How do you see the invisible struggle?
invitation_questions: How do you spot students who are masking?

thread_title: My school claims inclusion but nothing is actually inclusive
thread_description: We have 'inclusive' branding, disabled students, but no accommodations, no teacher training, no accessible facilities. It's tokenism, not inclusion.
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for actual inclusion in schools claiming to be inclusive?

thread_title: I need to balance one neurodivergent student's needs against the class
thread_description: Allowing fidget tools for one student means others want them. Extended time for exams means logistical chaos. Accommodation for one creates perceived 'unfairness' for many. How do I navigate this?
invitation_questions: How do you explain accommodations to a class without resentment building?

thread_title: Student has been tested but school won't act on results
thread_description: Formal ADHD diagnosis, psych report recommends accommodations. School: 'We don't have resources to implement this.' Family doesn't have power to push. Where's the accountability?
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for action when diagnosis exists but school resists?

thread_title: I'm teaching a skill but the system doesn't value it
thread_description: I focus on: problem-solving, resilience, understanding strengths/challenges, building agency. Tests value: rote memorization, compliance, speed. The system ignores what actually matters.
invitation_questions: How do you teach what matters when the system measures what doesn't?

thread_title: My school blames parents for everything and I disagree
thread_description: Struggling student: school says 'Bad parenting, lack of support at home.' Reality: parent is doing everything possible with limited resources and their child is neurodivergent. Blame is misplaced.
invitation_questions: How do you shift school culture from parent-blame to collaboration?

thread_title: Differentiation sounds great on paper but I have no time
thread_description: I'm supposed to differentiate for 40 kids across 3 levels. I have 40 minutes to teach, grade 100 notebooks, fill out administrative forms. Differentiation requires 48-hour days.
invitation_questions: How do you do meaningful differentiation with real-world constraints?

thread_title: My state doesn't recognize the diagnosis (or it's new)
thread_description: Student diagnosed with ADHD but my state education system still calls it 'behavioral problem.' Or they diagnose with outdated criteria. Official recognition doesn't exist. How do I work around this?
invitation_questions: How do you support students in systems that don't formally recognize their diagnosis?

thread_title: Teacher training needs to change completely
thread_description: Teachers learn behavior management but not neurodevelopment. They learn one pedagogy but not differentiation. Then we're supposed to teach neurodivergent kids. The training is broken.
opening_story: System trains teachers for average students.
invitation_questions: What would actually help teacher training change?

thread_title: I see potential but the system crushes it
thread_description: Student: creative, problem-solver, interesting thinker. System: needs to sit still, memorize, follow rigid rules. By year 3, they're disengaged. I watch potential get crushed.
invitation_questions: How do you preserve a neurodivergent student's spark in an inflexible system?

thread_title: My students' parents are fighting the diagnosis I suspect
thread_description: Parent emails: 'My child is fine,' 'This is all diet,' 'Stop giving excuses for laziness.' They're in denial. Child is struggling. How do I communicate without creating conflict?
invitation_questions: How do you discuss concerns when parents are resistant?

thread_title: How do I find a clinician who actually knows ADHD/autism?
thread_description: Called 8 doctors. One said girls don't have ADHD. One wanted to immediately medicate. One told me to 'just parent better.' Finding a good assessor is harder than getting the diagnosis.
invitation_questions: How did you find a good clinician? What questions did you ask? What red flags did you notice?

thread_title: The evaluation cost ₹25,000 and I'm not sure it was thorough
thread_description: Got a private evaluation. Comprehensive (I think?). Cost more than my monthly therapy budget. Clinician didn't ask about family history, school history, home behavior. Was this thorough or just expensive?
invitation_questions: What does a good evaluation actually include? How do you know if you got what you paid for?

thread_title: My child is on ADHD medication and I'm terrified
thread_description: Meds help them focus but I'm terrified of side effects. Is appetite loss normal? Should we be monitoring anything? How do I know if they're okay?
invitation_questions: What's a realistic side effect profile? How do you monitor safely?

thread_title: The psychiatrist and psychologist gave different diagnoses
thread_description: Psychiatrist: 'ADHD.' Psychologist: 'Anxiety with ADHD features.' Therapist: 'Both but anxiety is primary.' Who's right? Which diagnosis do I treat?
invitation_questions: How do you navigate conflicting professional opinions?

thread_title: Medication side effects are worse than the condition
thread_description: ADHD meds help focus but: reduced appetite (child losing weight), sleep disruption (nightmares), emotional flatness. Child says meds make them feel 'not like themselves.' Do we keep going or try something else?
invitation_questions: How do you decide if medication benefits outweigh side effects?

thread_title: Everyone has different opinions on medication and I'm paralyzed
thread_description: Mom: 'Don't medicate,' 'You'll ruin their brain.' Doctor: 'Medication is evidence-based.' Therapist: 'Try behavioral first.' Parent groups: mixed opinions. How do I choose?
opening_story: Too many voices, no clear answer.
invitation_questions: How did you make the medication decision? What actually helped you decide?

thread_title: I think I have it too but I'm too scared to pursue diagnosis
thread_description: Reading about my kid's diagnosis, so much clicks for me. But getting diagnosed = confronting my own limitations, losing the denial that's kept me functioning.
invitation_questions: Did you get your own diagnosis after your child's? What shifted?

thread_title: Genetic counseling seems pointless but also crucial
thread_description: Should we pursue genetic testing? What will we do with the information? Will it predict severity for other kids? Can insurance use it against us?
invitation_questions: How do you decide about genetic testing? What did you learn that mattered?

thread_title: My child's diagnosis scared my extended family
thread_description: Got diagnosis for my 8yo. Told family (needed for school accommodations). Now: 'Will they be able to have relationships?' 'Can they get married?' 'What about their future?' Their catastrophizing is adding to my anxiety.
invitation_questions: How do you manage family members' fears about your child's diagnosis?

thread_title: The therapist is great but also expensive and I can't sustain it
thread_description: Therapist is perfect. My kid is improving. Cost: ₹2500/session, 2x/week. I can't sustain this. What do I do when I can't afford the help that's working?
invitation_questions: How have you found sustainable therapy options?

thread_title: Nobody told me what to expect after diagnosis
thread_description: Got the diagnosis. Now what? Do we tell school immediately? Should we try accommodations first? What if medication is needed? Timeline is unclear.
opening_story: Diagnosis is answer but not roadmap.
invitation_questions: After diagnosis, what was your next step? What resources helped?

thread_title: My child's doctor disagrees with my parenting strategies
thread_description: I let my kid stim (help them regulate). Doctor: 'Stop that, it's a behavior problem.' I want to validate their diagnosis. Doctor: 'You're enabling.' We're on opposite pages.
invitation_questions: How do you navigate disagreement with your child's doctor?

thread_title: I can't find a therapist (in my city, within budget)
thread_description: 40-minute drive to nearest specialist. Cost ₹3000/session. 6-month wait list. Urban area but still no access. What do I do?
invitation_questions: How have you accessed therapy when it's geographically/financially impossible?

thread_title: Health insurance won't cover diagnosis or therapy
thread_description: Policy covers 'mental health' but doesn't list ADHD/autism as eligible. Fought with insurance. Still won't cover. Cost comes entirely out-of-pocket.
invitation_questions: How are you managing costs when insurance won't cover?

thread_title: My child's health history is fragmented across providers
thread_description: No one provider has the full picture. Pediatrician doesn't talk to therapist. Neuropsych evaluated separately. School has different assessment. Information silos.
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for coordinated care?

thread_title: I'm worried medication will change my child's personality
thread_description: ADHD meds will help them focus but what if it makes them less 'them'? What if they become emotionally flat? How do I weigh focus against personality?
invitation_questions: Did your child's personality change on meds? How do you know if change is growth or loss?

thread_title: Diagnosis came late and I'm angry at healthcare system
thread_description: My 14yo just diagnosed ADHD. Looking back, signs were obvious at 7, 10, 12. Multiple doctors missed it. Years of struggle that could've been prevented.
invitation_questions: How do you process anger about missed diagnosis?

thread_title: I need to choose: medication or therapy (I can't afford both)
thread_description: Therapist and doctor both recommend: medication + therapy. Reality: ₹8000/month total for both. I can do one. Which matters more?
invitation_questions: If you had to choose one, which did you prioritize? What was the outcome?

thread_title: My daughter is undiagnosed because she masks perfectly
thread_description: High achiever, well-liked, no visible struggle. But at home: anxiety, sensory meltdowns, perfectionism that's crushing. Doctors: 'She seems fine.' How do I get evaluation for invisible suffering?
invitation_questions: How do you advocate for diagnosis when your child 'seems fine'?

thread_title: I want diagnosis but my child refuses to be labeled
thread_description: Child (12yo) has clear symptoms. We've been to evaluation. Diagnosis would help. But they refuse: 'I'm not broken,' 'I don't want a label,' 'Everyone's a little like this.'
opening_story: Child autonomy vs. access to support.
invitation_questions: How do you honor your child's feelings while supporting them?

thread_title: My resume has gaps and employers see 'red flag'
thread_description: Left job for burnout/mental health. Was unemployed 8 months. Now applying and every employer fixates on the gap. How do I explain it without oversharing?
invitation_questions: How did you reframe employment gaps? What language actually worked?

thread_title: Interview anxiety destroys me (even though I'm qualified)
thread_description: Phone screen: mind goes blank. In-person: can't make eye contact, ramble, forget what I wanted to say. Assessment tests: fail even when I'd pass on the job. How do I get past interviews?
opening_story: Competence doesn't translate to interview performance.
invitation_questions: What interview hacks actually work? How do you manage anxiety?

thread_title: Atypical Advantage / Nagarro TestingPro exists but I didn't know
thread_description: Finally heard about companies with neuro-affirming hiring. How do I apply? How are they different from normal recruitment?
invitation_questions: Anyone familiar with Atypical Advantage or Nagarro TestingPro? How does the process differ?

thread_title: Online assessments are my kryptonite
thread_description: Most companies use automated assessments (reasoning, speed, timed). ADHD brain: time pressure = panic. Autism brain: questions that are too abstract. I fail tests I shouldn't.
invitation_questions: How do you handle automated assessments? Any strategies or companies that don't use them?

thread_title: I don't know if I should disclose or hide my diagnosis
thread_description: Should I mention ADHD/autism in the application? During interview? After hiring? When to disclose determines everything but I don't know the right timing.
invitation_questions: When did you disclose (if at all)? How did you choose?

thread_title: My neurodiversity is actually a job skill in my niche
thread_description: ADHD + pattern-recognition = I'm exceptional at data analysis. Autism + detail focus = quality assurance excellence. The right role leverages my neurodiversity, not despite it.
opening_story: Difference is strength in the right context.
invitation_questions: What role lets your neurodiversity be an asset, not a liability?

thread_title: I've built freelance work and it's perfect—but also precarious
thread_description: I'm thriving: choose projects, set my pace, hyperfocus on what matters. No commute, no masking, sustainable pace. But income is unstable and I have no benefits.
invitation_questions: How do you make freelance work sustainable as a neurodivergent person?

thread_title: Group interviews are impossible for me
thread_description: Many companies do group assessments: solve problem together, present, chat. ADHD brain: can't follow multiple conversations. Autism brain: group dynamics = paralysis. I miss opportunities.
invitation_questions: How do you handle group interviews?

thread_title: I need to reframe my disability as strength (but it's hard)
thread_description: Cover letter needs to spin gaps, job-hopping, struggles as 'resilience' and 'adaptability.' But it feels like lying when the truth is 'I was struggling and didn't know why.'
invitation_questions: How do you authentically reframe your story in a way employers buy?

thread_title: Startup culture is hostile even though it seems 'progressive'
thread_description: Flat hierarchy = no structure (ADHD crisis). 'Passion over process' = no documentation (needs not met). Startup parties = sensory nightmare. Progressive doesn't mean neurodivergent-friendly.
invitation_questions: What company cultures actually work for neurodivergent employees?

thread_title: I have no professional network and job hunting is isolating
thread_description: Networking is: remember names, keep in touch, navigate social dynamics. ADHD + autism brain = I'm invisible. But jobs go to people with networks.
opening_story: System rewards connections I can't make.
invitation_questions: How do you job search without a network? How do you build one?

thread_title: My 'job history' looks chaotic but it makes sense to me
thread_description: 5 jobs in 6 years sounds flaky. But each was: mismatch, burnout, exit. Pattern is invisible unless you know neurodivergence. How do I explain the pattern without disclosing?
invitation_questions: How do you explain job-hopping in a way that makes sense to employers?

thread_title: Remote work is non-negotiable for me but companies want office
thread_description: Remote is: no commute overload, flexible environment, control over sensory input. In-office = crisis. Many companies are 'flexible' but not actually.
invitation_questions: How do you find fully remote roles? How do you negotiate remote work?

thread_title: I'm good at one thing but bad at 'professionalism'
thread_description: I can do the job. Excellence is there. But I'm 'not a good cultural fit' because I: don't small-talk, dress differently, skip unnecessary meetings, communicate directly.
invitation_questions: How do you navigate 'culture fit' when you're neurodivergent?

thread_title: I'm overqualified but they won't hire me for it
thread_description: I apply for jobs slightly below my ability (lower stress). Employers see resume and say 'You'll get bored.' Or they fear I'll leave. Catch-22: can't get hired for right-level job, can't get hired for lower-level job.
invitation_questions: How do you address overqualification concerns?

thread_title: Referrals are how jobs get filled and I have zero network
thread_description: Most hiring in India happens through referrals. I don't have those networks. Online applications get screened out. I'm locked out of the system.
invitation_questions: How do you find jobs without referrals? How do you build referral-able relationships?

thread_title: I need structure but they want 'independent contractors'
thread_description: Many companies want gig workers (no benefits, high flexibility, independent). But I need structure, clarity, routine. Gig work = anxiety for me.
invitation_questions: How do you find structured work in a gig economy world?

thread_title: I'm in burnout and job hunting feels impossible
thread_description: Last job destroyed me. Currently recovering. Know I need work but can't imagine managing interview, onboarding, in-office life. How do I job search while recovering?
invitation_questions: How do you re-enter the job market post-burnout?

thread_title: The job exists but I can't get hired for it
thread_description: I can do data analysis, design, coding—the actual work. But hiring systems (resume screening, interviews, group assessments) are hostile. Stuck.
invitation_questions: How do you bypass hiring barriers when you know you can do the job?

thread_title: I finally found a role that works (and want to share why)
thread_description: After years of job-hopping, I found a role that: has clear expectations, values depth over speed, supports focus time, is mission-driven. Here's how I got here and why it matters.
invitation_questions: What made your current/past job actually work? What's different?
